# arpdvark

> ARP network scanner for Linux. Discovers devices on the local subnet, resolves hostnames, identifies hardware vendors, and supports persistent device labels.

## Quick start

Requires Linux and root/CAP_NET_RAW. Install from source:

    git clone https://github.com/fabioconcina/arpdvark
    cd arpdvark && make build
    sudo ./arpdvark

## CLI modes

### Interactive TUI (default)

    sudo arpdvark [-i <interface>] [-t <seconds>] [--large]

Full-screen terminal UI with auto-refresh, keyboard navigation, and label editing.

### JSON output (--json)

    sudo arpdvark --json [-i <interface>] [--large]

Single scan, JSON array to stdout. Suitable for piping to jq, scripts, or other tools.

Output format:

    [
      {
        "ip": "192.168.1.1",
        "mac": "aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff",
        "vendor": "Cisco Systems",
        "hostname": "router.local",
        "label": "main-router",
        "first_seen": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z",
        "last_seen": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
      }
    ]

### Human-readable table (--once)

    sudo arpdvark --once [-i <interface>] [--large]

Single scan, tab-aligned text table to stdout. Parseable with awk/cut.

### MCP server (--mcp)

    arpdvark --mcp

Runs an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server on stdio. Exposes a `scan_network` tool.

Tool: scan_network
  Parameters:
    - interface (string, optional): network interface name (auto-detected if omitted)
    - allow_large (boolean, optional): allow subnets larger than /16 (default: false)
  Returns: JSON array of device objects (same schema as --json output)

Claude Desktop configuration example:

    {
      "mcpServers": {
        "arpdvark": {
          "command": "sudo",
          "args": ["/path/to/arpdvark", "--mcp"]
        }
      }
    }

## Exit codes

- 0: success (devices found)
- 1: error (permissions, interface not found, scan failure)
- 2: scan completed but no devices found (--json and --once only)

## Flags

    -i <interface>  Network interface (default: auto-detect)
    -t <seconds>    Refresh interval for TUI mode (default: 10)
    --large         Allow subnets larger than /16
    --json          Single scan, JSON output, exit
    --once          Single scan, text table output, exit
    --mcp           Run as MCP server (stdio)
    --version       Print version and exit

## Permissions

## Scanning behavior

The first ARP sweep sends 3 rounds of requests (100 ms apart) to catch slow responders
such as Wi-Fi clients in power-save mode. Subsequent scans send a single round since
the device table accumulates across sweeps.

## Permissions

Uses raw ARP sockets (AF_PACKET). Requires sudo or CAP_NET_RAW:

    sudo setcap cap_net_raw+ep ./arpdvark

## Labels

Devices can be labeled by MAC address. Labels persist in ~/.config/arpdvark/tags.json
and are included in all output modes (TUI, JSON, table, MCP).
